item.title: The Comfort of the Dashboard
item.description:
The Comfort of the DashboardOn Mistaking the Map for the TerritoryThe word comes from horse-drawn carriages.A dashboard was a protective plank mounted at the front of the carriage to deflect the mud and debris kicked up by hooves. Not a display. Not…

Read the full article on Substack

item.title: The World as a Feed
item.description:
There is a specific moment that happens to most people now, and most people do not register it as remarkable.You are sitting with someone. A conversation is occurring. You are, by most definitions, paying attention. And then something shifts. The…

item.title: Jerome: When Translation Became Doctrine
item.description:

item.title: Can Ghosts Get Pregnant?
item.description:
nowThe internet pretends to be fast. Always moving, always discarding, constantly reinventing itself. A meme burns through Twitter in hours, an app rockets from nowhere to a billion-dollar valuation, and then evaporates before anyone remembers to…

Read the full article on Substack
